name: bx-Blogger Default
author: bx-Blogger Team
description: Clean, responsive default theme. Bootstrap 5 via CDN, custom styles compiled from SCSS via Vite into public/themes/bx-blogger-default/assets/.

options.label: Top-bar Logo (rendered to the left of the site title; leave unset for no logo)
Top-bar Logo — Dark Mode variant (optional; if set, swapped in when the site is in dark mode)
Hide site title text (logo only). Falls back to showing the title if no logo is set, so the brand area is never empty.
Favicon (browser tab icon — PNG / SVG / ICO all work)
Site Title (top bar — leave blank to use the site-wide name from System Settings)
Top-bar Title Color (Light Mode)
Top-bar Title Color (Dark Mode)
Accent Color (Light Mode) — overrides Bootstrap's --bs-primary across buttons, links, focus rings
Accent Color (Dark Mode) — same as above but for dark mode
Posts Per Page
Home Page Intro Page (renders above the post grid; replaces the default site-name title block)
Show Featured Posts section on the home page (above the regular post grid)
Featured Posts Count (number of pinned posts to render in the featured section)
Blog Index Intro Page (/blog — renders between header and post grid)
Tags Listing Intro Page (/tags — renders between header and tag cloud)
Categories Listing Intro Page (/categories — renders between header and category cards)
Search Page Intro Page (/search — renders between the search form and results)
Custom 404 Page (replaces the stock "Page not found" message)
Show post excerpt on post cards
Show author name on post cards
Show publish date on post cards
Show estimated reading time on post cards
Show Categories link in main menu (under Blog)
Show Tags link in main menu (under Blog)
Show bx-Blogger logo + copyright in footer (left side)
Custom Footer Text (shown in place of the bx-Blogger logo + copyright when the option above is OFF)
Show Authors link in footer (before the RSS link)
Show RSS link in footer
Custom CSS (injected into <head> after the theme's own stylesheet — overrides win without !important)
Custom <head> HTML (analytics / verification — injected verbatim, UNSANITIZED, admin-trust required)
